key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 free cash flow $4.6B divided by 623M weighted average diluted shares (10-K filed 2026-02-12)
FY2025 nights +8% and GBV +12%; Q1 2026 revenue +18% and nights +9%; modest take-rate and ADR lift

open_questions: Confirm live price before sizing; `live_price_confirmed: false` in `valuation.json`
Monitor city-level STR bans and lodging-tax accruals (10-K: **37,000** jurisdictions in Q1 2026 vs **33,000** FY2025)
No third-party sources indexed; re-run scan when Substacks or HK material added
Dual-class structure: review proxy for founder control and SBC trend
thesis: Airbnb operates a global two-sided marketplace connecting hosts who list homes and experiences with guests who book short-term stays and activities. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$12.2 billion** (up **10%** versus last year) on **533 million** Nights and Seats Booked and **$91.3 billion** of Gross Booking Value. **[Fact]** The company is asset-light: it does not own the properties on its platform and earns primarily from service fees on guest and host transactions. **[Fact]** FY2025 free cash flow was **$4.6 billion**; cash and cash equivalents were **$6.9 billion** at year-end. **[Inference]** Owner economics are driven by booking volume, average daily rate, and take rate, with seasonality tied to global travel patterns.
why_market_wrong: There is **no classic dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** The market often values Airbnb as a post-pandemic travel recovery story, while the business has shifted toward a durable, capital-light toll on global nights with rising marketing and stock-based compensation masking GAAP earnings. **[Fact]** Q1 2026 revenue grew **18%** year over year even as free cash flow was flat at **$1.7 billion**, reflecting timing of unearned fees versus GBV. **[Inference]** Generalists may focus on net income (**$2.5 billion** in FY2025, down **5%**) and miss that cash generation remains strong. **[Inference]** Bears may underweight the **$3.8 billion** of 2025 share repurchases and **$5.6 billion** remaining authorization as evidence of aligned capital return at scale.
